informal_statement.text: Given a binary search tree whose in-order traversal is sorted strictly ascending and an integer key, return a new BST identical to the input on every existing key plus an additional node with the given key, unless the key is already present, in which case the tree is returned unchanged.
preconditions: the input tree is a valid BST (in-order traversal is strictly ascending)
required_properties: the output tree is a valid BST
the multiset of keys in the output equals the multiset of keys in the input union {key} if the key was absent, else equals the multiset of keys in the input
every key present in the input is also present in the output
edge_cases: single-node tree, key less than root
single-node tree, key greater than root
single-node tree, key equal to root (unchanged)

semantic_units.description: BST precondition: the input tree is a valid BST (in-order strictly ascending).
BST invariant preservation: the output tree is also a valid BST.
common_failure_modes: asserting it only when the key is absent

semantic_units.description: Key-set change: the output's key multiset is the input's multiset union {key} if the key was absent, else equals the input's multiset.
common_failure_modes: asserting insertion regardless of duplicates (breaks BST uniqueness)

semantic_units.description: Duplicate no-op: inserting an existing key returns a tree with the same key multiset.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (t : Tree) (k : Int), IsBst t → IsBst (bstInsert t k)
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If the input tree is a valid BST, the output tree preserves the BST invariant.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (t : Tree) (k : Int), IsBst t → (k ∈ keys t ∨ (keys (bstInsert t k)).Perm (k :: keys t))
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If the input tree is a valid BST and the key was absent, the output's keys are the input's keys plus the new key.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (t : Tree) (k : Int), IsBst t → k ∈ keys t → (keys (bstInsert t k)).Perm (keys t)
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If the input tree is a valid BST and the key was already present, the output's keys are a permutation of the input's.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem bst_insert_terminates (t : Tree) (k : Int) : ∃ n : Nat, True := by trivial
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The recursive definition is well-founded on the structure of the input tree (i.e., recursion terminates).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bst_insert_shape (t : Tree) (k : Int) :
  match t with
  | Tree.nil => bst_insert t k = Tree.node Tree.nil k Tree.nil
  | Tree.node l x r =>
      match k.cmp x with
      | .lt => bst_insert t k = Tree.node (bst_insert l k) x r
      | .gt => bst_insert t k = Tree.node l x (bst_insert r k)
      | .eq => bst_insert t k = t
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Unfolding the implementation: on Nil it creates a single-node tree; on Node it recurses left/right depending on comparison, and leaves the tree unchanged on duplicates.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bst_insert_noop_on_duplicate (t : Tree) (k : Int) :
  (∃ l r, t = Tree.node l k r) → bst_insert t k = t
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If the tree already contains a node with key k at the root, insertion of k is a no-op (the implementation returns the original node).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bst_insert_preserves_bst (t : Tree) (k : Int) :
  BST t → BST (bst_insert t k)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If the input tree satisfies the binary search tree ordering invariant, then the output tree also satisfies it.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bst_insert_ordering (t : Tree) (k : Int) :
  BST t →
  (∀ x, x ∈ keys (bst_insert t k) →
    (x < rootKey (bst_insert t k) ∨ x = rootKey (bst_insert t k) ∨ x > rootKey (bst_insert t k)))
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: All keys in the resulting BST respect the BST ordering relative to the root (capturing that insertion places the new key in the correct side).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bst_insert_mem_inserted (t : Tree) (k : Int) :
  BST t → k ∈ keys (bst_insert t k)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: After insertion, the key k is present in the resulting tree (either newly inserted or already existed, in which case the tree is unchanged).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bst_insert_mem_preserves_others (t : Tree) (k : Int) :
  BST t →
  (∀ x, x ≠ k → x ∈ keys t → x ∈ keys (bst_insert t k))
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Insertion does not remove any existing key other than possibly leaving it unchanged; all keys different from k remain present.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bst_insert_no_new_duplicate (t : Tree) (k : Int) :
  BST t →
  (countKeys (bst_insert t k) k = countKeys t k)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Because the implementation is a no-op on duplicates, the number of occurrences of k does not increase.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bst_insert_correct_by_cases (t : Tree) (k : Int) :
  match t with
  | Tree.nil => True
  | Tree.node l x r =>
      (k < x → bst_insert t k = Tree.node (bst_insert l k) x r) ∧
      (k > x → bst_insert t k = Tree.node l x (bst_insert r k)) ∧
      (k = x → bst_insert t k = t)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The output matches the intended recursive BST insertion behavior for all comparison cases.
